summary: A news website called 'Inside Pet Tech' dedicated to the Pet Tech industry. Modeled after Retail Dive, it features news stories, articles, interviews, and videos about companies, technology, and ideas reshaping pet care. Includes a strong back-end content moderation system for easy article uploads. Target audience: founders, executives, veterinarians, investors, and operators. Categories are being simplified to: Startups & Funding, News & Views, Interviews, and Market Trends.
architectural_notes: Site should follow an editorial news layout inspired by Retail Dive
Content management system needed for back-end article upload and moderation
Admin/sign-in page must not be publicly linked or visible in navigation — only accessible to insiders and reporters
Core editorial categories simplified to: Startups & Funding, News & Views, Interviews, Market Trends
known_issues: About page and Contact page are not visibly accessible/navigable from the public site
News & Views category section is missing from the homepage bottom category sections (only Startups & Funding, Interviews, and Market Trends appear)

existing_features.title: Build a public-facing homepage modeled after Retail Dive's editorial layout. It must include: a top navigation bar with the Inside Pet Tech logo and category links, a hero/featured story section showcasing the most recent featured article with large image and headline, a 'Latest News' grid of recent published articles (thumbnail, category tag, headline, summary, author, date), a 'Feature Stories' horizontal scroll or section, a 'Videos' section if video articles exist, and a footer with site description, category links, and tagline 'Covering the Companies, Technology, and Ideas Reshaping Pet Care'.

Create individual category pages for each of the six categories: Startups & Funding, AI & Data, Veterinary Tech, Connected Devices, Market Trends, and Interviews. Each page shows a filterable list of published articles in that category with thumbnail, headline, summary, author, and date.

Build a full article detail page. It must display: the article headline, author, published date, category tag, content type badge (Article / Feature Story / Interview / Video), hero image or embedded video player (for Video type), and full body content. Include a 'Related Articles' section at the bottom showing 3 articles from the same category.

Build an admin dashboard at /admin, protected by Internet Identity login. The dashboard must include: an article list table showing all articles (all statuses) with columns for title, category, contentType, status, publishedAt, and action buttons (Edit, Publish, Archive, Delete); a 'Create New Article' form; and an article editor form with all fields including a rich text body editor, featured toggle, status selector, and thumbnail URL input.

Apply a consistent editorial visual theme across the entire site inspired by the Inside Pet Tech brand. Use the logo's gradient palette (deep crimson-to-indigo) as accent colors against a clean off-white/light gray background with dark near-black text. Typography should use a bold serif or semi-serif for headlines and a clean sans-serif for body text. Layout should feel authoritative and editorial — similar to a professional B2B trade publication. Apply this theme consistently to navigation, cards, category tags, buttons, and the admin dashboard.

Render the Inside Pet Tech logo uploaded by the user as a static asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/inside-pet-tech-logo.png and use it in the site navbar and any other appropriate branding locations.

Replace the top hero/video section on the homepage with a generic featured article section. Instead of only displaying video content types, it should display the most recent featured article regardless of content type (Article, Feature Story, Interview, or Video). The section should show the article's thumbnail image, category tag, headline, summary, author, and published date in a large editorial hero layout. If the featured article is of type Video, the thumbnail should still be used (no forced video embed at the top).

Create an About page at /about describing Inside Pet Tech, its mission, editorial focus, and target audience (founders, executives, veterinarians, investors, and operators in the pet tech industry). Style it consistently with the site's editorial theme.

Create a Contact page at /contact with publication contact information and a contact form. The form should collect name, email, subject, and message fields with basic client-side validation. Style it consistently with the site's editorial theme.

Add a newsletter signup section to the site footer (or as a dedicated section just above the footer). The section should allow visitors to enter their email address and subscribe. Store newsletter subscriber emails in the backend with a timestamp. Expose a backend function to subscribe an email and a function to list all subscribers (admin-only).
Add About and Contact page links to the site footer's navigation links section, alongside existing category links.

Add visible navigation links to the About page (/about) and Contact page (/contact) in the site's top navigation bar (Navbar) so visitors can easily discover and access these pages from any page on the site.

Remove the Internet Identity sign-in button and any admin login entry points from all public-facing navigation (Navbar, Footer, and any public pages). The admin/sign-in flow should only be reachable by directly navigating to /admin — it must not be linked, displayed, or discoverable by general site visitors.

Add a brief mention of advertising opportunities to the Contact page (/contact). This should appear as a short paragraph or callout within the existing contact information section, noting that a variety of advertising opportunities are available and inviting interested parties to reach out for more information.

Simplify the editorial categories from six to four core categories: 'Startups & Funding', 'News & Views', 'Interviews', and 'Market Trends'. Remove 'AI & Data', 'Veterinary Tech', 'Connected Devices', and 'Videos' as standalone categories. Update the backend Category enum to reflect only these four categories. Update all frontend references including the top navigation bar category links, footer category links, category pages, article editor category dropdown, and any category label/slug lookup maps in utils.

Add a 'News & Views' category section to the homepage bottom sections so that all four categories — Startups & Funding, News & Views, Interviews, and Market Trends — are displayed. The News & Views section must follow the same layout and styling as the other three category sections (section header with 'More' link, article cards with thumbnail, category tag, headline, and date).

new_feature_requests.summary: Feature stories and articles section covering pet tech industry
Interviews section with industry executives and thought leaders
Video content section
Back-end content management and moderation system for uploading and managing articles
Homepage with editorial layout similar to Retail Dive, featuring latest news and featured stories
Category pages covering topics: startups/funding, AI/data, veterinary tech, connected devices, market trends

Create a backend data model and actor with full CRUD operations for Articles. Each article must have: id, title, slug, summary, body (rich text), author, category (enum: Startups & Funding | AI & Data | Veterinary Tech | Connected Devices | Market Trends | Interviews | Videos), contentType (enum: Article | Feature Story | Interview | Video), thumbnailUrl, videoUrl (optional), publishedAt (timestamp), status (Draft | Published | Archived), and featured (bool) fields.

Build a backend admin authentication system using Internet Identity. Admin-only functions (create, update, delete, publish, archive articles) must verify the caller is an authorized principal. Provide a function to add/remove admin principals.

Seed the backend with at least 6 sample published articles spanning all categories and content types (articles, feature story, interview, video) so the homepage and category pages render populated content on first load.
Change the top hero/video section on the homepage to a generic featured article section, not restricted to video content types — any featured article can appear there.

Ensure the backend Motoko actor fully supports all admin operations required by the admin dashboard: create, update, delete, publish, and archive articles. Verify that role-based access control (admin principal checks) is enforced on all mutating operations. Expose a function to add and remove admin principals. All operations must be callable from the frontend via the generated actor interface.

Seed the backend with at least 6 sample published articles spanning all categories (Startups & Funding, AI & Data, Veterinary Tech, Connected Devices, Market Trends, Interviews) and content types (Article, Feature Story, Interview, Video). At least one article must have the 'featured' flag set to true so the homepage hero section renders populated content on first load. Sample data should include realistic pet tech industry titles, summaries, authors, thumbnail URLs, and published timestamps.
Create an About page describing Inside Pet Tech, its mission, and target audience.
Create a Contact page with a contact form or contact information for the publication.
Add a newsletter signup section at the bottom of the site (footer or dedicated section) allowing visitors to subscribe with their email.

Ensure the backend Motoko actor fully supports all admin content management operations: create, update, delete, publish, and archive articles. Enforce role-based access control (admin principal checks) on all mutating operations. Expose addAdmin and removeAdmin functions. Seed the backend with at least 6 sample published articles spanning all categories (Startups & Funding, AI & Data, Veterinary Tech, Connected Devices, Market Trends, Interviews) and content types (Article, Feature Story, Interview, Video), with at least one article marked as featured, realistic pet tech titles/summaries/authors, and plausible thumbnail URLs.
Make the sign-in/admin login page non-public (hidden from general visitors). It should only be accessible to insiders and reporters, not linked or visible in public navigation.
Add a mention of advertising opportunities to the Contact page.

Simplify editorial categories to four core categories: Startups & Funding, News & Views, Interviews, and Market Trends. Update all category references across the site (navigation, category pages, article forms, backend enums, and seed data).
Ensure 'News & Views' category section appears in the homepage bottom sections alongside Startups & Funding, Interviews, and Market Trends.
